stories.title: Starmer Defends £15bn Defence Investment Plan at PMQs
stories.summary: PM Keir Starmer defended his government's £15bn defence investment plan at PMQs, calling it the biggest military funding increase in 40 years. Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch accused him of leaving a £5bn funding gap.

stories.title: Andy Burnham Poised to Succeed Starmer as Prime Minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is widely expected to win the Labour leadership contest later this month, succeeding Sir Keir Starmer as Prime Minister. Angela Rayner publicly backed Burnham's devolution agenda.

stories.title: Guardian PMQs Live: No 10 Defends Defence Funding as 'Credible'
stories.summary: No 10 insisted the defence investment plan was 'credible' as Badenoch urged incoming PM Burnham to condemn it. Ministers and MPs raised concerns about cuts to road projects to fund the plan.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs, Debates & Legislation

stories.title: PMQs: Badenoch Presses Starmer on Defence Spending Shortfall
stories.summary: A tense PMQs exchange saw Kemi Badenoch challenge Starmer over an alleged £5bn gap in the £15bn defence investment plan. The PM defended the package as historic, while a Guardian sketch described a stroppy Starmer unafraid to push back.

stories.title: Eve Samson Appointed as Next Clerk of the House of Commons
stories.summary: UK Parliament announced Eve Samson as the incoming Clerk of the House of Commons, one of Parliament's most senior administrative roles.

stories.title: Reform UK Raises Illegal Migration Detention Policy in Commons
stories.summary: Reform UK used parliamentary time to push its position that every illegal migrant should be detained, denied asylum, and deported — the PM rejected the approach at the dispatch box.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy & internal politics

stories.title: Burnham Urged to Adopt Radical Economic Policies to See Off Reform UK
stories.summary: An exclusive Guardian poll shows Andy Burnham should pursue bold economic policies — including rent control and higher wealth taxes — to protect key Labour seats from Reform UK at the next general election.

stories.title: Angela Rayner Backs Burnham's Devolution Vision
stories.summary: Former Deputy PM Angela Rayner offered public support for Andy Burnham's devolution agenda, suggesting the outgoing Starmer government had too often defended the status quo and left the impression of insufficient decentralisation.

stories.title: Badenoch Accuses Starmer of Leaving Defence Spending 'Mess'
stories.summary: Tory leader Kemi Badenoch accused the PM of leaving a £5bn hole in defence funding, despite Starmer's announcement of £15bn in new military investment — the biggest such increase in 40 years.

stories.title: Tory MPs Furious as Road Projects Cut to Fund Defence Plan
stories.summary: Conservative MPs Robert Jenrick (Newark) and Hamish Falconer (Lincoln) are reported to be furious after road improvement projects in their constituencies were cut to fund the government's defence investment plan.

stories.title: Farage Faces Pressure Over Undisclosed Properties in £4m Portfolio
stories.summary: A Times investigation revealed Nigel Farage owns five mortgage-free homes but only declared two to Parliament, prompting fresh calls for him to clarify his housing arrangements and raising transparency concerns.

stories.title: Analysis: Is Farage Losing His Political 'Aura'?
stories.summary: A New Statesman analysis suggests Reform UK is losing momentum after a run of by-election disappointments, including Andy Burnham's win in Makerfield — a seat where Reform had made significant council gains.

stories.title: Swinney Criticises Burnham for 'Lack of Knowledge' About Scotland
stories.summary: Scottish First Minister John Swinney accused Labour leadership hopeful Andy Burnham of showing a lack of knowledge about Scotland after Burnham suggested power north of the border was too centralised. Swinney described the comments as 'polemics'.

stories.title: Commons Officials: No Evidence Murrell Stole Taxpayer Money
stories.summary: House of Commons officials signalled no reason to believe former SNP CEO Peter Murrell misappropriated taxpayer funds, with the Clerk of the House confirming no concerns had been raised.

stories.title: Kate Forbes, Christian Conscience and Scotland's New Culture War
stories.summary: Analysis explores how Deputy First Minister Kate Forbes's Christianity continues to generate public debate in Scotland, almost a year after she announced plans to leave frontline politics.

stories.title: 'Remaking the State' — Government Sets Out Public Sector Reform Agenda
stories.summary: A minister delivered a speech at the Re:State Annual Conference in London on 1 July 2026, setting out the government's agenda for remaking public services and state institutions.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results & polls

stories.title: Reform UK Suffers Run of By-Election Disappointments
stories.summary: Reports indicate Reform UK has experienced a series of disappointing by-election results in recent weeks, including a loss in Makerfield — a constituency where the party had previously made strong council gains.

stories.title: Lords Committee Challenges Government's Earned Settlement Plans
stories.summary: A House of Lords committee published a report challenging the government's proposed 'earned settlement' scheme, raising concerns about its implementation — though the report does not block the government from proceeding.

stories.title: Lords Debate Bill Targeting Foreign State-Linked Organisations
stories.summary: The House of Lords debated amendments to a Bill designed to address the threat posed by foreign state-linked organisations operating in the UK (30 June 2026).
